wiki.title: Liquidation Levels
wiki.summary: Highlights likely liquidation zones (forced close levels) based on leverage assumptions and price swings.

text.content: Liquidation Levels is a TradingView indicator designed to provide actionable context for decision-making (bias, timing, and risk framing).

rich_text.plain_text: Liquidation Levels is a TradingView indicator designed to provide actionable context for decision-making (bias, timing, and risk framing).

text.content: Highlights likely liquidation zones (forced close levels) based on leverage assumptions and price swings.

rich_text.plain_text: Highlights likely liquidation zones (forced close levels) based on leverage assumptions and price swings.

text.content: Timeframe(s): choose execution TF and any higher TF(s) used for context

rich_text.plain_text: Timeframe(s): choose execution TF and any higher TF(s) used for context

text.content: Smoothing/Signal: secondary smoothing or signal line length (if present)

rich_text.plain_text: Smoothing/Signal: secondary smoothing or signal line length (if present)

text.content: Thresholds: OB/OS bands or trend trigger levels (if present)

rich_text.plain_text: Thresholds: OB/OS bands or trend trigger levels (if present)

text.content: Alerts: enable/disable and pick trigger conditions (if available)

rich_text.plain_text: Alerts: enable/disable and pick trigger conditions (if available)

text.content: Treat liquidation levels as potential “liquidity targets” where stops and forced liquidations may cluster.

rich_text.plain_text: Treat liquidation levels as potential “liquidity targets” where stops and forced liquidations may cluster.

text.content: Levels above price can act as upside magnets in squeeze conditions; levels below price can act as downside magnets during risk-off.

rich_text.plain_text: Levels above price can act as upside magnets in squeeze conditions; levels below price can act as downside magnets during risk-off.

text.content: Best used with structure (range highs/lows, PDH/PDL, VWAP, etc.) to avoid chasing every printed level.

rich_text.plain_text: Best used with structure (range highs/lows, PDH/PDL, VWAP, etc.) to avoid chasing every printed level.

text.content: Start with higher timeframe context (bias first).

rich_text.plain_text: Start with higher timeframe context (bias first).

text.content: Wait for a clean trigger on the execution timeframe (avoid mid-range chop).

rich_text.plain_text: Wait for a clean trigger on the execution timeframe (avoid mid-range chop).

text.content: Use the indicator as a filter + timing tool, not a standalone “buy/sell machine.”

rich_text.plain_text: Use the indicator as a filter + timing tool, not a standalone “buy/sell machine.”

text.content: Plan invalidation before entry (where the setup is wrong).

rich_text.plain_text: Plan invalidation before entry (where the setup is wrong).

text.content: Confirm signal alignment across timeframes (or across a dashboard scan).

rich_text.plain_text: Confirm signal alignment across timeframes (or across a dashboard scan).

text.content: Add a second confirmation (structure, key level reaction, volume/flow, or volatility).

rich_text.plain_text: Add a second confirmation (structure, key level reaction, volume/flow, or volatility).

text.content: Scale/trim into targets rather than expecting perfect tops/bottoms.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale/trim into targets rather than expecting perfect tops/bottoms.

text.content: Log outcomes by regime (trend vs range) and tune settings only after a sample size.

rich_text.plain_text: Log outcomes by regime (trend vs range) and tune settings only after a sample size.

text.content: All indicator signals lag to some degree; faster settings increase noise.

rich_text.plain_text: All indicator signals lag to some degree; faster settings increase noise.

text.content: Range-bound periods can produce repeated false triggers without a regime filter.

rich_text.plain_text: Range-bound periods can produce repeated false triggers without a regime filter.

text.content: Do not treat plotted levels as guaranteed support/resistance; manage risk explicitly.

rich_text.plain_text: Do not treat plotted levels as guaranteed support/resistance; manage risk explicitly.

text.content: Backtests/alerts can behave differently due to realtime bar updates and repaint-like effects (depending on implementation).

rich_text.plain_text: Backtests/alerts can behave differently due to realtime bar updates and repaint-like effects (depending on implementation).

wiki.title: WaveTrend Dashboard
wiki.summary: Dashboard-style WaveTrend readouts across symbols/timeframes for scanning and alignment.

text.content: The WaveTrend Dashboard indicator is a multi-symbol momentum scanner that displays WaveTrend oscillator values across four timeframes (5min, 15min, 1H, 4H) for up to nine assets simultaneously. It replaces traditional RSI-based scanning with the WaveTrend algorithm, providing faster identification of overbought and oversold conditions across your entire watchlist at a glance.

rich_text.plain_text: The WaveTrend Dashboard indicator is a multi-symbol momentum scanner that displays WaveTrend oscillator values across four timeframes (5min, 15min, 1H, 4H) for up to nine assets simultaneously. It replaces traditional RSI-based scanning with the WaveTrend algorithm, providing faster identification of overbought and oversold conditions across your entire watchlist at a glance.

text.content: Each column represents one asset from your watchlist. Each row represents a timeframe: 5-minute, 15-minute, 1-hour, and 4-hour. Every cell displays two elements:

rich_text.plain_text: Each column represents one asset from your watchlist. Each row represents a timeframe: 5-minute, 15-minute, 1-hour, and 4-hour. Every cell displays two elements:

text.content: A WaveTrend value with a trend arrow — ▲ (bullish) when WT1 is above WT2, or ▼ (bearish) when WT1 is below WT2

rich_text.plain_text: A WaveTrend value with a trend arrow — ▲ (bullish) when WT1 is above WT2, or ▼ (bearish) when WT1 is below WT2

text.content: A percentage showing the price change for that timeframe's current bar

rich_text.plain_text: A percentage showing the price change for that timeframe's current bar

text.content: The WaveTrend value displayed is the WT1 line (fast line) rounded to the nearest integer. Values typically range from -100 to +100, with readings above the overbought level (default: 53) indicating overextended bullish momentum and readings below the oversold level (default: -53) indicating overextended bearish momentum.

rich_text.plain_text: The WaveTrend value displayed is the WT1 line (fast line) rounded to the nearest integer. Values typically range from -100 to +100, with readings above the overbought level (default: 53) indicating overextended bullish momentum and readings below the oversold level (default: -53) indicating overextended bearish momentum.

text.content: Cells remain neutral (dark) unless the WaveTrend value crosses above the overbought level (red highlight) or below the oversold level (green highlight). This mode provides the cleanest view for identifying extreme conditions at a glance.

rich_text.plain_text: Cells remain neutral (dark) unless the WaveTrend value crosses above the overbought level (red highlight) or below the oversold level (green highlight). This mode provides the cleanest view for identifying extreme conditions at a glance.

text.content: A full-spectrum gradient from blue (deeply oversold) through green (neutral) to red (deeply overbought). Every cell is continuously colored based on relative WaveTrend intensity, giving a complete momentum picture.

rich_text.plain_text: A full-spectrum gradient from blue (deeply oversold) through green (neutral) to red (deeply overbought). Every cell is continuously colored based on relative WaveTrend intensity, giving a complete momentum picture.

text.content: A two-tone gradient where green indicates oversold conditions and red indicates overbought conditions, with intensity proportional to the WaveTrend value.

rich_text.plain_text: A two-tone gradient where green indicates oversold conditions and red indicates overbought conditions, with intensity proportional to the WaveTrend value.

text.content: The ▲ and ▼ arrows reflect the relationship between the WaveTrend fast line (WT1) and signal line (WT2). When WT1 crosses above WT2, bullish momentum is building and an up arrow is displayed. When WT1 crosses below WT2, bearish momentum is building and a down arrow is displayed. Arrows provide directional context that complements the absolute WaveTrend value — a bearish arrow in overbought territory, for example, may suggest an early reversal.

rich_text.plain_text: The ▲ and ▼ arrows reflect the relationship between the WaveTrend fast line (WT1) and signal line (WT2). When WT1 crosses above WT2, bullish momentum is building and an up arrow is displayed. When WT1 crosses below WT2, bearish momentum is building and a down arrow is displayed. Arrows provide directional context that complements the absolute WaveTrend value — a bearish arrow in overbought territory, for example, may suggest an early reversal.

text.content: The WaveTrend oscillator is computed independently for each symbol on each timeframe using request.security(). The algorithm follows five steps:

rich_text.plain_text: The WaveTrend oscillator is computed independently for each symbol on each timeframe using request.security(). The algorithm follows five steps:

text.content: 1 — An EMA of the typical price (HLC3) is calculated over the channel length

rich_text.plain_text: 1 — An EMA of the typical price (HLC3) is calculated over the channel length

text.content: 2 — The absolute deviation from this EMA is smoothed with another EMA of the same length

rich_text.plain_text: 2 — The absolute deviation from this EMA is smoothed with another EMA of the same length

text.content: 3 — The normalized difference (CI) is computed as (HLC3 - ESA) / (0.015 x D)

rich_text.plain_text: 3 — The normalized difference (CI) is computed as (HLC3 - ESA) / (0.015 x D)

text.content: 4 — WT1 is the EMA of CI over the average length

rich_text.plain_text: 4 — WT1 is the EMA of CI over the average length

text.content: 5 — WT2 is the 4-period SMA of WT1 for crossover detection

rich_text.plain_text: 5 — WT2 is the 4-period SMA of WT1 for crossover detection

text.content: This produces an oscillator that responds more quickly to momentum shifts than standard RSI while maintaining smooth, readable output across all timeframes.

rich_text.plain_text: This produces an oscillator that responds more quickly to momentum shifts than standard RSI while maintaining smooth, readable output across all timeframes.

text.content: For Heatmap and Red/Green modes, the raw WaveTrend value is mapped from its native range of approximately -100 to +100 onto a 0-100 scale using the formula: mapped = (WT + 100) / 2. This mapped value drives the color gradient calculations. For OB/OS mode, colors are determined by direct comparison against the configurable overbought and oversold thresholds without any mapping.

rich_text.plain_text: For Heatmap and Red/Green modes, the raw WaveTrend value is mapped from its native range of approximately -100 to +100 onto a 0-100 scale using the formula: mapped = (WT + 100) / 2. This mapped value drives the color gradient calculations. For OB/OS mode, colors are determined by direct comparison against the configurable overbought and oversold thresholds without any mapping.

text.content: Each symbol requires four request.security() calls (one per timeframe), each returning the WT1 value, WT2 value, and price change in a single tuple. With nine symbols, this totals 36 security calls, leaving headroom within TradingView's 40-call limit per indicator.

rich_text.plain_text: Each symbol requires four request.security() calls (one per timeframe), each returning the WT1 value, WT2 value, and price change in a single tuple. With nine symbols, this totals 36 security calls, leaving headroom within TradingView's 40-call limit per indicator.

text.content: EMA period for the price channel (default: 10). Higher values produce a smoother, less reactive oscillator.

rich_text.plain_text: EMA period for the price channel (default: 10). Higher values produce a smoother, less reactive oscillator.

text.content: EMA period for the final CI smoothing (default: 21). Controls WT1 responsiveness to momentum changes.

rich_text.plain_text: EMA period for the final CI smoothing (default: 21). Controls WT1 responsiveness to momentum changes.

text.content: Threshold above which cells are highlighted as overbought in OB/OS mode (default: 53).

rich_text.plain_text: Threshold above which cells are highlighted as overbought in OB/OS mode (default: 53).

text.content: Threshold below which cells are highlighted as oversold in OB/OS mode (default: -53).

rich_text.plain_text: Threshold below which cells are highlighted as oversold in OB/OS mode (default: -53).

text.content: Configurable ticker symbols for each column. The default watchlist includes BTC, SOL, ETH, SUI, HYPE, BNB, XRP, TAO, and ZEC perpetual contracts on Binance. Any valid TradingView symbol can be substituted.

rich_text.plain_text: Configurable ticker symbols for each column. The default watchlist includes BTC, SOL, ETH, SUI, HYPE, BNB, XRP, TAO, and ZEC perpetual contracts on Binance. Any valid TradingView symbol can be substituted.

text.content: Choose between Heatmap (full gradient), Red / Green (two-tone), or OB/OS (highlight extremes only).

rich_text.plain_text: Choose between Heatmap (full gradient), Red / Green (two-tone), or OB/OS (highlight extremes only).

text.content: Tiny, Small, or Medium text sizing for the table. Smaller sizes fit more information on screen; larger sizes improve readability.

rich_text.plain_text: Tiny, Small, or Medium text sizing for the table. Smaller sizes fit more information on screen; larger sizes improve readability.

text.content: The WaveTrend algorithm used is the standard EMA → deviation → normalized CI → smoothed WT1/WT2 formula. The source code is protected because it contains the specific implementation of: (1) The multi-symbol request.security() architecture that computes WT1, WT2, and price change for nine symbols across four timeframes (36 total security calls, optimized to stay within TradingView's 40-call limit per indicator) with tuple-based data packing. (2) The three-mode color mapping system — OB/OS uses threshold comparison, Heatmap uses a linear (WT + 100) / 2 normalization mapped to a multi-stop color gradient, and Red/Green uses a two-tone proportional mapping. (3) The trend arrow logic that evaluates WT1 vs WT2 crossover state independently for each cell.

rich_text.plain_text: The WaveTrend algorithm used is the standard EMA → deviation → normalized CI → smoothed WT1/WT2 formula. The source code is protected because it contains the specific implementation of: (1) The multi-symbol request.security() architecture that computes WT1, WT2, and price change for nine symbols across four timeframes (36 total security calls, optimized to stay within TradingView's 40-call limit per indicator) with tuple-based data packing. (2) The three-mode color mapping system — OB/OS uses threshold comparison, Heatmap uses a linear (WT + 100) / 2 normalization mapped to a multi-stop color gradient, and Red/Green uses a two-tone proportional mapping. (3) The trend arrow logic that evaluates WT1 vs WT2 crossover state independently for each cell.

text.content: This indicator displays momentum readings for informational purposes and does not generate trade signals or predict price direction. Overbought and oversold conditions do not guarantee reversals. It does not constitute financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

rich_text.plain_text: This indicator displays momentum readings for informational purposes and does not generate trade signals or predict price direction. Overbought and oversold conditions do not guarantee reversals. It does not constitute financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

wiki.title: Synthetic Max Pain
wiki.summary: Estimates a “max pain”/pinning zone using a synthetic model (not official options OI).

text.content: indicator adapts options max pain theory to perpetual futures and leveraged crypto markets by constructing a synthetic open interest model. Since TradingView does not provide strike-level open interest data for derivatives, this indicator generates a modeled strike ladder centered on each anchor period's opening price, distributes synthetic OI across strikes using a

rich_text.plain_text: indicator adapts options max pain theory to perpetual futures and leveraged crypto markets by constructing a synthetic open interest model. Since TradingView does not provide strike-level open interest data for derivatives, this indicator generates a modeled strike ladder centered on each anchor period's opening price, distributes synthetic OI across strikes using a

text.content: with configurable skew parameters, then applies standard option payoff calculations to identify the price levels where the most positioned capital would face maximum loss.

rich_text.plain_text: with configurable skew parameters, then applies standard option payoff calculations to identify the price levels where the most positioned capital would face maximum loss.

text.content: This approach — applying Gaussian-weighted synthetic OI modeling with option payoff mathematics to non-options markets — is an original analytical method. No standard open-source Pine Script indicator performs this calculation.

rich_text.plain_text: This approach — applying Gaussian-weighted synthetic OI modeling with option payoff mathematics to non-options markets — is an original analytical method. No standard open-source Pine Script indicator performs this calculation.

text.content: The indicator generates a configurable number of strikes (default: 41) spaced at a configurable increment (or auto-calculated at ~1% of price). Rather than assuming uniform OI across strikes, it distributes modeled OI using a

rich_text.plain_text: The indicator generates a configurable number of strikes (default: 41) spaced at a configurable increment (or auto-calculated at ~1% of price). Rather than assuming uniform OI across strikes, it distributes modeled OI using a

text.content: centered near the anchor price but offset from at-the-money to simulate realistic positioning patterns. An additional weighting boost is applied to slightly out-of-the-money strikes, where empirical positioning data shows retail and leveraged traders tend to cluster.

rich_text.plain_text: centered near the anchor price but offset from at-the-money to simulate realistic positioning patterns. An additional weighting boost is applied to slightly out-of-the-money strikes, where empirical positioning data shows retail and leveraged traders tend to cluster.

text.content: ) allow the model to reflect directional market lean.

rich_text.plain_text: ) allow the model to reflect directional market lean.

text.content: Amplifies the modeled short-side OI, pushing the MP Long (support) level further from the anchor.

rich_text.plain_text: Amplifies the modeled short-side OI, pushing the MP Long (support) level further from the anchor.

text.content:
Amplifies long-side OI.
These parameters model the asymmetric positioning seen in trending markets where one side of the book is heavier than the other.

rich_text.plain_text:
Amplifies long-side OI.
These parameters model the asymmetric positioning seen in trending markets where one side of the book is heavier than the other.

text.content: ) each maintain their own strike ladder, OI distribution, and max pain calculation. Each resets when its anchor period begins, using that period's opening price as the new center. When multiple anchors produce levels at similar prices, the confluence identifies stronger zones.

text.content: This indicator uses a synthetic model to estimate max pain levels. It does not use actual exchange open interest data at the strike level. The modeled levels are approximations based on statistical distributions and should be used as one factor among many in a trading analysis framework. It does not constitute financial advice and past performance does not guarantee future results.

The modeled levels are approximations based on statistical distributions and should be used as one factor among many in a trading analysis framework. It does not constitute financial advice and past performance does not guarantee future results.

wiki.title: HeikenAshi Trend
wiki.summary: Heikin Ashi-based trend state to reduce noise and keep you in moves longer.

text.content: HeikenAshi Trend is a TradingView indicator designed to provide actionable context for decision-making (bias, timing, and risk framing).

rich_text.plain_text: HeikenAshi Trend is a TradingView indicator designed to provide actionable context for decision-making (bias, timing, and risk framing).

text.content: Heikin Ashi-based trend state to reduce noise and keep you in moves longer.

rich_text.plain_text: Heikin Ashi-based trend state to reduce noise and keep you in moves longer.

text.content: Timeframe(s): choose execution TF and any higher TF(s) used for context

rich_text.plain_text: Timeframe(s): choose execution TF and any higher TF(s) used for context

text.content: Smoothing/Signal: secondary smoothing or signal line length (if present)

rich_text.plain_text: Smoothing/Signal: secondary smoothing or signal line length (if present)

rich_text.plain_text: Thresholds: OB/OS bands or trend trigger levels (if present)

text.content: Alerts: enable/disable and pick trigger conditions (if available)

rich_text.plain_text: Alerts: enable/disable and pick trigger conditions (if available)

text.content: Use HA trend state to stay in the move; exit/trim when HA trend flips or loses momentum.

rich_text.plain_text: Use HA trend state to stay in the move; exit/trim when HA trend flips or loses momentum.

text.content: Because HA smooths, entries are typically later but whipsaws are reduced compared to raw candles.

rich_text.plain_text: Because HA smooths, entries are typically later but whipsaws are reduced compared to raw candles.

text.content: Combine with a key level or breakout/retest to avoid late entries in extended moves.

rich_text.plain_text: Combine with a key level or breakout/retest to avoid late entries in extended moves.

text.content: Start with higher timeframe context (bias first).

rich_text.plain_text: Start with higher timeframe context (bias first).

text.content: Wait for a clean trigger on the execution timeframe (avoid mid-range chop).

rich_text.plain_text: Wait for a clean trigger on the execution timeframe (avoid mid-range chop).

text.content: Use the indicator as a filter + timing tool, not a standalone “buy/sell machine.”

rich_text.plain_text: Use the indicator as a filter + timing tool, not a standalone “buy/sell machine.”

text.content: Plan invalidation before entry (where the setup is wrong).

rich_text.plain_text: Plan invalidation before entry (where the setup is wrong).

text.content: Confirm signal alignment across timeframes (or across a dashboard scan).

rich_text.plain_text: Confirm signal alignment across timeframes (or across a dashboard scan).

text.content: Add a second confirmation (structure, key level reaction, volume/flow, or volatility).

rich_text.plain_text: Add a second confirmation (structure, key level reaction, volume/flow, or volatility).

text.content: Scale/trim into targets rather than expecting perfect tops/bottoms.

rich_text.plain_text: Scale/trim into targets rather than expecting perfect tops/bottoms.

text.content: Log outcomes by regime (trend vs range) and tune settings only after a sample size.

rich_text.plain_text: Log outcomes by regime (trend vs range) and tune settings only after a sample size.

text.content: All indicator signals lag to some degree; faster settings increase noise.

rich_text.plain_text: All indicator signals lag to some degree; faster settings increase noise.

text.content: Range-bound periods can produce repeated false triggers without a regime filter.

rich_text.plain_text: Range-bound periods can produce repeated false triggers without a regime filter.

text.content: Do not treat plotted levels as guaranteed support/resistance; manage risk explicitly.

rich_text.plain_text: Do not treat plotted levels as guaranteed support/resistance; manage risk explicitly.

text.content: Backtests/alerts can behave differently due to realtime bar updates and repaint-like effects (depending on implementation).

rich_text.plain_text: Backtests/alerts can behave differently due to realtime bar updates and repaint-like effects (depending on implementation).

$$DEMA = 2 \times EMA(source, length) - EMA(EMA(source, length), length)$$
This significantly reduces lag compared to a standard EMA.

text.content:
The raw CVD is standardized using the following formula:
$$Z = \frac{CVD - SMA(CVD, n)}{\sigma(CVD, n)}$$
Where $n$ is the lookback period and $\sigma$ is the standard deviation. A smoothing EMA is then applied to reduce noise.

rich_text.plain_text:
The raw CVD is standardized using the following formula:
$$Z = \frac{CVD - SMA(CVD, n)}{\sigma(CVD, n)}$$
Where $n$ is the lookback period and $\sigma$ is the standard deviation. A smoothing EMA is then applied to reduce noise.
